Let \( x \) be the number of residents who own all four items. Using the principle of inclusion-exclusion for four sets:

\[
\text{Total} = \text{Diamond} + \text{Golf} + \text{Spade} + \text{Candy} - \binom{2}{1}(\text{Exactly 2}) - \binom{3}{1}(\text{Exactly 3}) + \binom{4}{2}x
\]

Plugging in the numbers:

\[
900 = 195 + 367 + 562 + 900 - 2 \times 437 - 3 \times 234 + 6x
\]

Solving for \( x \):

\[
900 = 2024 - 874 - 702 + 6x \implies 6x = 252 \implies x
